evals.prompt: Deploy this linked Next.js app on Vercel, then tell me how to inspect the preview and promote it if the smoke test passes.
evals.expected_output: Keeps the work in the Vercel lane, distinguishes preview deploy from promotion, and includes verification before promotion.
assertions: Response keeps the task in vercel-deploy rather than routing immediately to deployment-automation
Response distinguishes preview deployment from promotion to production
Response includes verification or inspection before promotion

evals.prompt: Our AI tool created a Vercel deployment for a customer. We need a claim URL so they can take ownership.
evals.expected_output: Uses the claim-deployment lane, mentions preview plus claim handoff, and does not present that path as ordinary linked-project deployment.
assertions: Response mentions claim deployment or claim URL explicitly
Response treats this as a distinct handoff flow rather than a generic deploy
Response preserves the bundled helper or claim-flow reference as the execution path

evals.prompt: Help me design a deployment pipeline with Docker images, GitHub Actions, rollback strategy, and staging environments.
evals.expected_output: Routes the request out to deployment-automation instead of keeping it in Vercel-specific execution.
assertions: Response routes this generic deployment-design request to deployment-automation
Response does not frame vercel-deploy as the primary skill for Docker or GitHub Actions pipeline design
Response preserves Vercel-specific scope rather than acting as a catch-all deployment skill
